commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Heads, or necks, to indicate that hey deserved to die. (Haydock)
The Syrians acted thus, when they came as supplicants. (Josephus, viii. 14.)
The son of Psammetichus, king of Egypt, was led in this manner to execution, with 2000 others. (Herodotus iii. 14.)
Bessus was conducted to Alexander with a chain round his neck. (Curt. vii.) (Calmet)
What a reverse of fortune do we here behold! (Salien)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
